page: Microsoft Azure D4ds_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the DDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The instance is configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, 16.0 GB of system memory, and a 48 MB L3 cache. It includes a bundled 154 GB local SSD and supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark data indicates moderate single-core performance alongside weaker multi-core scaling. The instance achieves average results in single-core tasks, such as a Geekbench single-core score of 1639.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 1896.58. However, multi-core benchmarks show limitations, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 3502.0 and a poor bzip3 multi-threaded decompression speed of 19.46 GB/sec. Memory performance is also constrained, with uncached large-block read and write bandwidths measuring 29.78 GB/sec and 25.32 GB/sec respectively.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D4ds_v5 offers a balanced resource density for general-purpose workloads that require local scratch space without demanding high-throughput multi-core processing. The inclusion of a 154 GB local disk provides value for applications needing fast temporary storage. This instance is best suited for workloads such as static web serving, where it achieves a throughput of 6.57 GB/sec, basic text processing, light database operations, and small-scale single-threaded applications.
description: Microsoft Azure D4ds_v5 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine from the DDSv5 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 154 GB local SSD. Performance benchmarks reveal a clear tradeoff: the instance delivers average single-core performance, as seen in its Geekbench single-core score of 1639.0, but exhibits weaker multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth. For instance, its multi-core Geekbench score is 3502.0, and large-block read bandwidth is limited to 29.78 GB/sec. Qualitatively, the D4ds_v5 provides a balanced resource profile with cost-effective local storage for workloads that do not require intensive parallel processing. It is well-suited for static web serving, light database operations, and general-purpose applications requiring moderate temporary disk space.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4ds_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 154 GB local SSD, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor for balanced workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4ds_v5 is an x86_64 general-purpose VM with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 154 GB local SSD, running on an Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and integrated local SSD storage for standard workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM
Bundled 154 GB local SSD storage with Premium Storage capability
Average single-core performance suitable for basic text and web serving tasks
Weaker multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth performance tradeoffs
Appropriate for static web serving, light database operations, and general utility workloads
